codeReview.summary: The feature adds a stale-row detection path and metadata writes, but it does not refresh the visible returning-player history after stale-only reanalysis and it can regress freshness metadata by overwriting latest-db fields with older stale matches processed later in the loop.

issues.description: The dashboard only reloads stored matches when `syncResult.analyzedCount > 0`. If the returning-player refresh only updates stale existing rows, `refreshedStaleCount` increases but `analyzedCount` stays 0, so the UI continues showing the stale pre-refresh summaries for the current search session. That misses VAL-FRESHNESS-003's requirement that visible history reflects refreshed derived data after search completes.

Every match-performance reanalysis overwrites `player_sync_metadata.latest_db_match_id/latest_db_match_created_at` with the currently processed match. During stale refresh, older stale rows can be processed after the actual newest DB row, causing metadata to move backward to an older match. Subsequent freshness logic that depends on latest-db metadata can become inaccurate, so the durable sync state is not maintained truthfully.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo library documents that returning-player freshness needs durable sync state, but it does not warn that stale-only refreshes must also trigger a visible stored-history reload and must preserve monotonic latest-db metadata when older rows are reprocessed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md` mentions bounded reconciliation and durable sync state in general terms, while the bug appears in `components/dashboard/lol-stats-dashboard.tsx:897` and `app/api/match-performance/route.ts:110`.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton, handoff, skill guidance, and commit `9c2d0f1`. The implementation correctly adds stale-row detection and a new stale-id route, but it fails VAL-FRESHNESS-003 because stale-only refreshes do not update the visible search result and because sync metadata can be rewritten to an older latest-db match during stale reanalysis.
